name: rpg-writers-room
description: A panel of expert critics for a campaign you have already built. Reads the Obsidian vault (lore, NPCs, factions, cities, locations, the campaign bible) and the session transcripts, then convenes five rubric-grounded lenses — the Alexandrian architect, the Lazy GM, the showrunner, the player advocate, and the dramaturg — to judge two different things with two different rubrics: the quality of the writing, and the quality of the conducting at the table. Every claim is anchored to a verbatim quote with a vault path or a transcript timecode; anything unsupported is rated as insufficient evidence rather than guessed. The orchestrator synthesizes consensus, surfaces disagreement as trade-offs instead of resolving it silently, and renders a self-contained HTML report with prioritized, constructive fixes. Critique only — it never generates campaign content, never edits the vault, and never claims to be an objective score.
